data.title: 'Innovation Not Price Cuts' Chinese Government Tells Discounting Domestic EV Makers
data.description:
Chinese EV makers such as BYD (BYDDY) and XPeng (XPEV) have been warned by their government to focus on innovation and not price cuts to keep the sector healthy.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

EV Makers Meeting

According to a report in the South China Morning Post, a high level meeting of the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, the National Development and Reform Commission and the State Administration for Market Regulation, gathered 17 major carmakers to "further regulate the competition order" in the sector.  In short, it was a call to move away from aggressive discounting and to focus instead on technological innovation, such as chips and self-driving systems, as the best form of competition.

It comes as domestic manufacturers try and tackle cooling consumer demand and the phasing out of long-standing government subsidies, by cutting prices and offering incentives such as zero-interest financing.

It is a tactic which doesn't always pay dividends. February data from China showed that BYD's sales dropped to 190,190 vehicles, down 41.1% year over year and 9.5% sequentially, while rival maker Geely's (GELYF) combined brands delivered 206,000 vehicles and held the top position for a second month.

A Volatile Market

Analysts said this showed that China's EV market remains highly competitive and volatile, with leadership changing quickly despite BYD having closed 2025 with record performance. It could, they said, intensify market scrutiny of BYD's demand sustainability, incentive structures, and the impact of ongoing price competition.

Indeed, the government believes this "price war" is seen as "destructive competition" that could destabilize the industry and squeeze profits. It could also, it fears, lead to deflationary pressures and hurt the sector's supply chain through issues such as delayed payments to suppliers.

It worries that some EVs could even go bust if the situation continues.

data.author: David Craik

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Retreats as Oil Climbs Higher; Iran Targets Energy Infrastructure
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading lower on Thursday as investors digest escalating tensions in the Middle East and rising oil prices.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Iran vowed to retaliate by targeting key oil infrastructure sites in the Middle East after Israel launched missiles on its South Pars gas field. Over the past 24 hours, Iran has carried out aerial attacks on Saudi Aramco's Samref refinery, Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City, and Kuwait Petroleum Corporation's Mina al-Ahmadi and Mina Abdullah refineries. Brent crude futures (BZ), the international benchmark for oil, are up by over 2% as the Strait of Hormuz remains at a near standstill.

To counter higher prices, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that the U.S. is considering lifting sanctions on Iranian oil and a second Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) release. Removing the sanctions would allow oil to "flow into Malaysia, Singapore, Indonesia, Japan, India — who have been good actors in this," he said.

Furthermore, President Trump is considering sending ground troops to Iran, according to Reuters. The troops could provide security to tankers crossing through the Strait of Hormuz or conduct an operation to secure Iran's stockpile of highly ​enriched uranium.

Elsewhere, the labor market remains in a slow-to-fire-and-hire environment. Initial jobless claims for the week ended March 14 fell by 8,000 to 205,000, below the consensus estimate of 215,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, rose by 10,000 to 1.857 million and were slightly above the estimate of 1.852 million. Initial jobless claims indicate recent layoffs, while continuing claims measure the number of people still receiving unemployment benefits and reflect ongoing unemployment.

The Dow Jones is down by 0.73% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: UBISOFT Entertainment Stock (UBSFY) Jumps on Tom Clancy Studio Layoffs
data.description:
UBISOFT Entertainment (UBSFY) stock was up on Thursday after the video game developer announced a new round of layoffs. This latest round of job cuts at UBISOFT Entertainment is focused on the company's Red Storm development studio, which is behind the Tom Clancy's Rainbow Six and Ghost Recon series.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

UBISOFT Entertainment has decided to keep Red Storm around, but not in a game development role. Instead, the developer is being shifted to a support role. This will see it provide IT and Snowdrop support. As a result, 105 game development roles at the studio were cut. While it's unfortunate for the developers, Red Storm had slowly lost steam over the years, largely due to UBISOFT Entertainment shifting development of Tom Clancy games to other studios.

UBISOFT Entertainment's layoffs don't come as too much of a surprise. The company is currently undergoing a major restructuring, as it shifts game development studios into creative houses. The company's change in operations came about after it received massive funding from Tencent (TCEHY) in November 2025, which granted the investor a  26.32% stake in Vantage Studios, the new creative house with IPs including Assassin's Creed, Far Cry, and Rainbow Six.

Ubisoft Stock Rallies Today

UBISOFT Entertainment stock was up 0.11% on Thursday but was still down 42.1% year-to-date. The company's stock has also fallen 69.18% over the past 12 months. This suggests that investor confidence is low despite the company's reorganization and investment from Tencent.
data.author: William White

data.title: SanDisk Stock (SNDK) Unchained After Flash Divorce — My Bullish Outlook
data.description:
I remain aggressively bullish on SanDisk (SNDK) stock as the great flash divorce is finally finalized, leaving the company unchained from the multi-billion-dollar anchor of its former parent Western Digital (WDC). It is also perfectly positioned to dominate the artificial intelligence (AI) memory supercycle. What we're seeing today is a structural breakout for a pure-play giant that is only now free to run as global NAND (Not AND) flash memory prices hit terminal velocity.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While the legal separation began a year ago, it was the massive $3 billion secondary exit just last month that truly liberated the stock's valuation from its decade-long "overhang."

The $3 Billion Ghost Finally Leaves the Room

For nearly a decade, owning SanDisk was an exercise in frustration, largely because the business was tethered to Western Digital's legacy hard-disk-drive (HDD) operations. Even after the official spin-off in early 2025, a massive psychological and technical "overhang" remained because Western Digital still held a multi-billion-dollar equity stake. For institutional investors, this was the ultimate red flag. Any significant rally in SNDK shares was met with the looming threat of a massive block sale that would flood the market and cap the stock's upside. That threat has finally been neutralized.

Last month, Western Digital completed a $3.17 billion secondary offering, liquidating its remaining 5.8 million shares. This was the "final divorce" the market had been waiting for. The offloading of this stake through a sophisticated debt-for-equity swap allowed Western Digital to clean up its balance sheet and remove the last anchor dragging on SanDisk's valuation. Without the constant selling pressure from a former parent company seeking an exit, SNDK's path to its true fundamental value is now entirely clear.
data.author: Nikolaos Sismanis

data.title: Why Is Gold Down 5% Today, 3/19/26?
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD) has failed to behave like a safe haven amid escalating tensions in the Middle East and is down by over 5% on Thursday. In addition, the precious metal usually benefits from expectations of higher inflation. Higher gas and oil prices from the U.S.-Iran war and disruptions to the Strait of Hormuz have raised the risk of inflation. However, gold has fallen 8% since the start of the conflict on February 28.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A major factor sending gold lower is falling rate cut odds. Gold tends to benefit from lower rates, as the opportunity cost of holding it relative to interest-bearing assets, like Treasuries, goes down.

Have high conviction in QQQ? Leverage your view

Gold Faces Headwinds from Profit-Taking and Strong U.S. Dollar

When the war began, the most favored outcome at 33.7% was for the Fed to cut rates twice this year, according to the CME FedWatch tool. Now, the most likely outcome is for zero rate cuts by year-end with 72.8% odds.

Furthermore, investors may be taking profits following a historic run in the precious metal. With the S&P 500 (SPX) down by 4% over the past month, capital could be rotating into the index and out of gold. Meanwhile, the U.S. Dollar Index has risen 1.75% during the past month, putting additional pressure on gold as a stronger dollar makes the metal more expensive for overseas buyers.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'AI Agents Will Replace a Huge Percentage' of Human Work, Says Circle (CRCL) CEO
data.description:
Speaking at the Economic Club of New York, Jeremy Allaire, the CEO of stablecoin company Circle (CRCL), said that "AI agents will replace a huge percentage of work that's currently performed by humans on a massive scale," especially in white-collar jobs. Interestingly, the data is pointing in a similar direction. Indeed, Goldman Sachs (GS) expects the U.S. unemployment rate to rise to 4.5% by the end of the year, partly due to AI.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, Block (XYZ) is cutting around 40% of its workforce, and Meta (META) is reportedly considering up to 20,000 job cuts as companies look to become more efficient using AI. According to Allaire, in an interview with Yahoo Finance, this creates a cycle where improving AI reduces the need for workers, which lowers spending, puts pressure on company profits, and pushes businesses to invest even more in AI. However, he also pointed out that AI could significantly increase productivity across industries, much like personal computers and the internet did in the past.

Even so, Allaire believes that this transition will not be smooth and will require major changes in how society is structured. While ideas like retraining programs or universal basic income are often discussed, he argues that they may be too simple for a shift of this scale. Instead, he believes that a rethink of the social and economic system is needed, especially since AI is a global technology that cannot be handled one country at a time. Still, he remains optimistic about younger workers, explaining that those who learn how to use AI tools can become far more productive and valuable.

Is CRCL Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CRCL stock based on 10 Buys, six Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average CRCL price target of $119.68 per share implies 4.8% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: How JPMorgan Chase (JPM) Is Betting Against Private Credit and Why It Could Get Messy
data.description:
Wall Street has turned on private credit.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Leading banks Goldman Sachs (GS) and JPMorgan Chase (JPM) are now offering their hedge fund clients new ways to bet against the $1.8 trillion private credit market. Specifically, the firms have assembled baskets of listed companies with exposure to the troubled private credit market and are allowing them to bet against those companies.

Goldman's indexes of private credit companies include one focused on European financial institutions that have private credit exposure, as well as a group of business development companies, and another comprised of alternative asset managers.

Big Problems Looming in Private Credit?

JPMorgan Chase's basket includes alternative managers and business development companies that have exposure to the opaque world of private credit. The new vehicles provided by Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan come as the private credit market faces pressure from a wave of investor redemptions.

Individual investors and institutions are getting increasingly nervous that lenders may be too heavily exposed to poorly run and financially unfit software companies, a sector that's undergoing rapid disruption from advances in artificial intelligence (AI).

Owing to the massive $1.8 trillion size of the private credit market, analysts say that a massive rupture in the sector could trigger a market meltdown similar to the 2008 financial crisis. Some analysts say that allowing hedge funds to bet against private credit could exacerbate current problems and lead to further redemptions and panic selling among smaller investors.

Is JPM Stock a Buy?

JPMorgan Chase's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 12 Buy and eight Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average JPM price target of $347.47 implies 20% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: How Did the SpaceX Rocket Launch Affect Space Stocks Today?
data.description:
SpaceX was a hot topic on Thursday after Elon Musk's rocket company performed a successful mission this morning. It succeeded in launching a Falcon 9 rocket carrying 29 Starlink satellites from Cape Canaveral SLC-40. The company's Falcon 9 first stage booster also landed on the company's drone ship in the Atlantic Ocean, completing its 29th mission. Space stocks are mixed following SpaceX's successful launch.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here's how some of the top space stocks have moved after the launch.

  • Rocket Lab USA (RKLB) stock was up 2.82% on Thursday morning.
  • AST SpaceMobile (ASTS) stock rallied 3.31% as of this writing.
  • Intuitive Machines (LUNR) stock slipped 5.19% today.
  • Virgin Galactic Holdings (SPCE) stock sank 2.71% on Thursday.

Intuitive Machines stock was down for reasons outside of the SpaceX launch. The company reported earnings for Q4 2025 this morning. Its results failed to impress investors, despite strong guidance for 2026.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade ASTS

Where Do Analysts Stand on These Space Stocks?

Turning to the TipRanks stock comparison tool, traders can see what analysts think about these space stocks. Intuitive Machines has the best analysts' consensus rating at Strong Buy, followed by Moderate Buy ratings for Rocket Lab USA and AST SpaceMobile, and a Hold rating for Virgin Galactic Holdings.

Looking at upside potential for these stocks, SPCE is the highest at 40.56%, followed by LUNR at 29.75%, RKLB at 25.38%, and ASTS at 5.2%.
